items.label: hypothesis_stated
items.evidence: Introduction: "we investigate whether SIRT1 levels in brain affect the Ab plaque formation, pathology, and cognitive decline in AD mouse model".

items.label: outcomes_described
items.evidence: Intro/Methods define outcomes: plaques (6E10), Ab by ELISA, behavior (fear conditioning; Morris water maze), GFAP gliosis, secretase activity assays.

items.label: patient_characteristics
items.evidence: Animal ages sometimes given (e.g., "5-month-old", "4 months"), but no sex, weight, inclusion/exclusion criteria, or full baseline characteristics.

items.label: interventions_described

items.label: confounders_described

items.label: main_findings_clear

items.label: random_variability

items.label: adverse_events

items.label: loss_to_followup

items.label: exact_p_values

items.label: subjects_representative

items.label: participants_representative
items.evidence: Animal study; participation/non-response not applicable in usual sense; all generated/purchased mice used per protocols (default yes for animal studies).

items.label: setting_representative
items.evidence: Standard preclinical setting/methods described: controlled housing ("25°C", "12:12 hr light/dark"), standard assays (ELISA, IHC, Morris water maze).

items.label: subjects_blinded

items.label: assessors_blinded

items.label: data_dredging_acknowledged

items.label: followup_adjusted

items.label: appropriate_stats

items.label: compliance_reliable

items.label: outcomes_valid

items.label: same_time_period

items.label: randomized

items.label: allocation_concealed

items.evidence: Results report quantified intensity with sample sizes: '20... vessels... Of each case... In total, 100 vessels were analyzed' and Fig.2P shows mean±SEM with group comparisons.

items.label: random_variability
items.evidence: Fig.2P: '+ mean SEM are shown' for LOX fluorescence intensity; Methods 2.1 reports ages and PMIs as mean±SD.

items.label: adverse_events
items.evidence: Post-mortem human tissue observational pathology study (no intervention administered); adverse events are not a meaningful concept here.

items.label: loss_to_followup
items.evidence: No longitudinal follow-up; tissue samples analyzed cross-sectionally from brain banks (Methods 2.1).

items.label: exact_p_values
items.evidence: Fig.2P reports thresholds only: '***pb0.001; n.s.=non-significant' (no exact p-values provided).

items.label: subjects_representative
items.evidence: Samples sourced from 'The Netherlands Brain Bank' and 'Leiden University Medical Center' (Methods 2.1) but selection criteria/representativeness of all AD/HCHWA-D cases not described.

items.label: participants_representative
items.evidence: Retrospective post-mortem tissue bank study; participation/non-response not applicable in the same way as prospective enrollment (Methods 2.1).

items.label: setting_representative
items.evidence: Standard academic neuropathology setting using established immunohistochemistry/confocal microscopy methods; tissue from recognized brain banks (Methods 2.1–2.3).

items.label: subjects_blinded
items.evidence: Deceased subjects; no intervention or participant behavior and no possibility of subject blinding in post-mortem tissue analysis.

items.label: assessors_blinded
items.evidence: No statement that analysts were blinded during vessel selection/intensity measurement; Methods 2.3 describes selecting vessels and quantifying LOX intensity without blinding procedures.

items.label: data_dredging_acknowledged
items.evidence: While aims are stated, there is no explicit statement that analyses were pre-specified vs exploratory; e.g., added co-IP noted but not labeled post-hoc (Methods 2.4/Results).

items.label: followup_adjusted
items.evidence: No follow-up time or longitudinal outcomes; all measurements are from single post-mortem samples (Methods 2.1–2.3).

items.label: appropriate_stats
items.evidence: Fig.2P: 'one-way ANOVA, followed by a Tukey post hoc test' used to compare LOX intensity across 3 groups—appropriate for continuous intensity comparisons.

items.label: compliance_reliable
items.evidence: No intervention requiring compliance; laboratory staining procedures applied to tissue sections (Methods 2.2–2.3).

items.label: outcomes_valid
items.evidence: Uses well-described immunohistochemistry with negative controls and established markers/antibodies (e.g., 'Negative controls... without the primary antibodies'; Aβ MAB5201, GFAP).

items.label: same_population
items.evidence: Controls/AD from Netherlands Brain Bank, but HCHWA-D tissue 'provided by the Leiden University Medical Center' (Methods 2.1), indicating different source populations.

items.label: same_time_period
items.evidence: No recruitment/collection years provided for control vs AD vs HCHWA-D tissue; Methods 2.1 lists sources but not time periods.

items.label: randomized
items.evidence: Observational comparison of diagnostic groups; no random assignment mentioned (Methods 2.1 describes tissue from control/AD/AD-CAA/HCHWA-D).

items.label: allocation_concealed
items.evidence: No randomization/allocation in this post-mortem observational study, so concealment cannot apply.

items.label: confounding_adjusted
items.evidence: No statistical adjustment for age/sex/PMI/pathology severity; analysis focuses on group comparisons via ANOVA (Fig.2P) without covariates.

items.label: losses_accounted
items.evidence: No follow-up or attrition; fixed set of brain samples analyzed (Methods 2.1).

items.label: sufficient_power
items.evidence: No sample size or power calculation reported; small groups (e.g., '5 control', '5 AD', '5 AD patients with CAA', '5 HCHWA-D') in Methods 2.1.

items.label: hypothesis_stated
items.evidence: “The present work aimed at testing in vivo the effect…IDIF—in Aβ deposition and other AD features…” and “hypothesis that restoring…TTR/Aβ binding can be a therapeutic avenue in AD.”

items.label: outcomes_described
items.evidence: Methods pre-specify outcomes: “Brain Aβ40 and Aβ42 levels determination”, “Aβ plaque burden”, “CSF and plasma Aβ42…”, and “Morris water maze test (MWM)” for cognition.

items.label: patient_characteristics
items.evidence: Animals described: “female mice aged 5 or 7 months” with genotypes “AD/TTR+/+…AD/TTR+/−”; housing conditions given (12-h light/dark, 22±2°C, etc.).

items.label: interventions_described
items.evidence: Chronic: “administered in the drinking water (2.8mg drug/rodent/day, 575ppm) over two months”; Acute: “28mg…for 3 days”; controls “given water alone”.

items.label: confounders_described
items.evidence: Some factors controlled (sex, age, genotype, housing), but no baseline table or distributions (e.g., weight, litter, cage) across groups; only Ns like “AD/TTR+/+ n=7…AD/TTR+/− n=9”.

items.label: main_findings_clear
items.evidence: Results report group Ns and outcomes with direction: e.g., Fig.5/6/7 show Aβ measures; MWM: “significantly shorter escape latencies…from the fifth day” with n per group stated in legends.

items.label: random_variability
items.evidence: Statistical section: “All data were expressed as mean±SEM.” Figures repeatedly note “Error bars represent SEM.”

items.label: adverse_events
items.evidence: No reporting of adverse effects/toxicity/welfare impact from IDIF (e.g., morbidity, weight loss); only procedures and endpoints described.

items.label: loss_to_followup
items.evidence: Initial group sizes given, but some assays use smaller Ns (e.g., “AD/TTR+/+ n=5–7”); no explanation of attrition/exclusions or characteristics of lost animals.

items.label: exact_p_values
items.evidence: P-values reported as thresholds only: “∗p<0.05; ∗∗p<0.01; ∗∗∗p<0.001” and “p<0.05 was considered significant”; no exact p=… values.

items.label: subjects_representative
items.evidence: Subjects are standard transgenic model from a defined colony: “generated by crossing…Jackson Laboratory…with TTR-null mice…to generate…littermates”; implies representative of that colony.

items.label: participants_representative
items.evidence: Animal study; no consent/participation process. Cohorts described as “littermates” assigned to treatment/control conditions.

items.label: setting_representative
items.evidence: Standard laboratory setting described (controlled housing, standard assays: ELISA, IHC, MWM); “All procedures…in accordance with National and European Union Guidelines”.

items.label: subjects_blinded
items.evidence: No statement that animals were blinded to treatment; drug delivered via drinking water could be detectable, but blinding is not described.

items.label: assessors_blinded
items.evidence: No mention that ELISA/IHC quantification or behavioral scoring was performed blinded to group; only describes methods and software (Image-Pro Plus, SMART).

items.label: data_dredging_acknowledged
items.evidence: Paper lists planned endpoints but does not state which analyses were pre-specified vs exploratory/post-hoc; e.g., multiple outcomes (Aβ, plaque, MWM, fluids) without plan declaration.

items.label: followup_adjusted
items.evidence: Follow-up duration fixed by design: chronic treatment “over two months” starting at 5 months and sacrifice at 7 months; same schedule for treated and controls.

items.label: appropriate_stats
items.evidence: Methods specify tests consistent with outcomes: “one way ANOVA with Bonferroni’s” for biochemical measures; “t-test” for MWM; normality checked by “D’Agostino and Pearson tests”.

items.label: compliance_reliable
items.evidence: Intervention via drinking water, but no measurement of intake or dose verification per mouse; only nominal dosing “mg drug/rodent/day, 575ppm”.

items.label: outcomes_valid
items.evidence: Validated methods used and detailed: ELISA kits for “Human Aβ40/42”, biotinylated “6E10” IHC, and established behavioral test “Morris water maze” with defined parameters.

items.label: same_population
items.evidence: Groups are within the same colony and described as “cohorts of littermates AD/TTR+/+ and AD/TTR+/−”; controls maintained “in the same conditions”.

items.label: same_time_period
items.evidence: Age at start is specified (5 months), but no explicit statement that all groups were run concurrently/blocked by cohort date; timing of experiments not reported.

items.label: randomized
items.evidence: No statement of random allocation to IDIF vs water; describes groups by genotype and treatment but does not mention randomization procedure.

items.label: allocation_concealed
items.evidence: Allocation concealment not described; and since randomization is not reported, concealment cannot be confirmed.

items.label: confounding_adjusted
items.evidence: No adjusted analyses reported (e.g., covariates, mixed models); comparisons mainly ANOVA/t-tests between groups without controlling for potential confounders beyond design restrictions.

items.label: losses_accounted
items.evidence: Different Ns appear across analyses (e.g., “n=5–7 per group”); paper does not report exclusions, missing samples, or whether losses could bias results.

items.label: sufficient_power
items.evidence: No sample size or power calculation; group sizes small (e.g., chronic AD/TTR+/+ n=7, AD/TTR+/− n=9) with multiple endpoints, raising risk of underpowering.

items.label: hypothesis_stated
items.evidence: Abstract: 'objective...to evaluate the existence of cognitive plateaus...during the course of Alzheimer's disease (AD).'

items.label: outcomes_described
items.evidence: Methods defines outcome: PCA-derived cognitive measure 'F1' and algorithm 'looking for stable periods of 3 years or longer.'

items.label: patient_characteristics
items.evidence: Methods: CERAD AD and controls; exclusions: '<3 years'; Table 1 reports %Male, age, education, and baseline cognitive test means/SDs.

items.label: interventions_described
items.evidence: Observational analysis of longitudinal cognitive testing; no intervention assigned ('historical patient group...CERAD').

items.label: confounders_described
items.evidence: Age/sex/education described (Table 1) and compared plateau vs non-plateau in Results, but no multivariable confounder set is specified.

items.label: main_findings_clear
items.evidence: Results: '22% of AD subjects (54/243)...exhibited a plateau' and gives plateau length 'mean 3.6 years; range 3 to 7 years.'

items.label: random_variability
items.evidence: Variability reported: 'standarderror, 3% for AD group'; plateau length range; baseline and group comparisons show means±SD.

items.label: adverse_events
items.evidence: No treatments administered; study uses existing CERAD neuropsychological assessments, so adverse events are not expected/relevant.

items.label: loss_to_followup
items.evidence: Discussion notes 'high dropout rate' and 'attrition rate...AD (78%)', but does not describe characteristics of those lost.

items.label: exact_p_values
items.evidence: Reports exact p-values, e.g., age difference 'P = .009'; education 'P = .021'; CDR difference 'P = .018'.

items.label: subjects_representative
items.evidence: Subjects from CERAD registry, but paper does not detail how CERAD sites sampled the broader AD population beyond general inclusion criteria citation.

items.label: participants_representative
items.evidence: Participation/non-response rate at enrollment not reported; only mentions many 'failed to return for follow-up' without enrollment refusal data.

items.label: setting_representative
items.evidence: Setting is multi-site CERAD ('Duke University, 1988–1996') but specific clinical settings/facilities are not described.

items.label: subjects_blinded
items.evidence: No intervention or treatment comparison requiring blinding; analysis of registry cognitive trajectories only.

items.label: assessors_blinded
items.evidence: Paper states subjects were evaluated with 'standardized instruments' but does not mention whether outcome assessors were blinded.

items.label: data_dredging_acknowledged
items.evidence: Study framed as method development: 'we propose a method...plateau-searching algorithm'; predefined plateau as 'stable period of 3 years or longer.'

items.label: followup_adjusted
items.evidence: Uses linear mixed model for repeated measures over time (equation 2) and algorithm uses observed timepoints; subjects have varying 'observation period'.

items.label: appropriate_stats
items.evidence: Methods use PCA to derive composite cognition (F1) and linear mixed models for longitudinal data; plateau algorithm defined in Appendix.

items.label: compliance_reliable
items.evidence: No intervention requiring compliance; outcomes are observational cognitive test scores from registry follow-ups.

items.label: outcomes_valid
items.evidence: Outcome components are established tests: MMSE (Folstein 1976), Boston Naming, verbal fluency, recognition; CERAD standardized assessment cited.

items.label: same_population
items.evidence: Both AD and controls drawn from same CERAD dataset ('1,094 subjects with AD and 463 controls...enrolled').

items.label: same_time_period
items.evidence: Methods: group 'recruited between 1987 and 1995' and evaluated 'at entry and annually thereafter' for both patients and controls.

items.label: randomized
items.evidence: Registry-based observational study; no allocation to intervention groups (AD vs NC is diagnosis-based).

items.label: allocation_concealed
items.evidence: No randomization or intervention assignment; therefore allocation concealment does not apply.

items.label: confounding_adjusted
items.evidence: Analyses mainly compare groups with t-tests and mixed-model slopes; no multivariable adjustment for age/education/CDR when estimating plateau prevalence.

items.label: losses_accounted
items.evidence: Large attrition acknowledged ('AD (78%)') but no sensitivity analyses/weighting; final sample restricted to those with 'at least three complete sets'.

items.label: sufficient_power
items.evidence: No sample size or power calculation reported; limitations note small numbers in some CDR stages ('small numbers...make it difficult to draw conclusions').
